Name: Kung Pao Chicken
Description: A classic Sichuan dish featuring diced chicken stir-fried with peanuts, dried chili peppers, and Sichuan peppercorns. The chicken is tender and the peanuts add a satisfying crunch, making it a perfect companion to steamed rice.

Name: Mapo Tofu
Description: A signature Sichuan dish with silky tofu cubes simmered in a spicy, aromatic sauce with minced beef and doubanjiang (fermented broad bean paste). Vibrant red, intensely flavorful, and wonderfully fragrant.

Name: Sichuan Boiled Fish
Description: Tender fish fillets poached in a fiery, aromatic chili oil broth with bean sprouts and dried chilies. Spicy, numbing, and intensely flavorful — a Chongqing specialty.

Name: Twice-Cooked Pork
Description: The king of Sichuan home cooking. Slices of pork belly are first boiled, then wok-fried with leeks, garlic sprouts, and doubanjiang. Richly aromatic with a lingering savory finish.

Name: Pickled Cabbage Fish
Description: Delicate fish fillets swim in a tangy, spicy broth with pickled mustard greens that add a refreshing sour crunch. Bright, appetizing, and irresistibly warming.

Name: Spicy Chicken
Description: Bite-sized chicken pieces fried until crispy outside and tender inside, then stir-fried with an abundance of dried chilies and Sichuan peppercorns for a fiery, numbing, and addictive flavor.

Name: Dan Dan Noodles
Description: A classic Sichuan street noodle dish topped with minced pork, preserved vegetables, and a complex spicy-sesame sauce. Every bite is a burst of numbing heat and savory richness.

Name: Fish-Fragrant Eggplant
Description: Despite its name, this dish contains no fish. Tender eggplant strips are stir-fried in a sweet, sour, and spicy garlic sauce that mimics the flavor profile of traditional fish dishes.

Name: Steamed Sea Bass
Description: Fresh sea bass steamed to perfection and finished with sizzling hot oil and soy sauce. The fish is delicate, succulent, and retains its natural sweetness.

Name: Poached Chicken
Description: Chicken poached to achieve smooth, glossy skin and tender, subtly sweet meat. Served with a classic ginger-scallion dipping sauce — an indispensable staple of Cantonese cuisine.

Name: Shrimp Dumplings
Description: The quintessential dim sum item. Translucent, delicate wrappers encase plump, fresh shrimp. The texture is pleasantly bouncy and each bite releases a burst of juicy, savory flavor.

Name: Stir-Fried Rice Noodles
Description: Wide rice noodles wok-fried over intense heat with beef and bean sprouts, developing that signature wok hei (breath of wok) for a smoky, deeply satisfying flavor.

Name: Char Siu (BBQ Pork)
Description: The crown jewel of Cantonese roasted meats. Pork marinated in a sweet-savory glaze and roasted until the exterior is beautifully glazed and the interior remains tender and juicy.

Name: Clay Pot Rice
Description: Rice cooked in a clay pot with cured meats, chicken, and vegetables. The bottom develops a golden, crispy crust while the top remains fluffy and aromatic.

Name: Wonton Noodle Soup
Description: Springy egg noodles in a clear, flavorful broth with plump shrimp wontons. A comforting Hong Kong classic that warms the soul with every spoonful.

Name: Sweet and Sour Pork
Description: Crispy battered pork pieces coated in a glossy sweet and sour sauce with pineapple and bell peppers. A beloved classic that balances tangy and sweet flavors perfectly.

Name: Peking Duck
Description: One of China's most internationally renowned dishes. Duck roasted until the skin is perfectly crisp and the meat is juicy and tender. Served with thin pancakes, sweet bean sauce, and shredded scallions.

Name: Zhajiang Noodles
Description: A classic Beijing noodle dish topped with a rich, savory sauce made from fermented soybean paste and minced pork, served with fresh shredded cucumber, radish, and bean sprouts.

Name: Mutton Hot Pot
Description: A traditional Beijing-style hot pot featuring thinly sliced mutton cooked in a copper pot with a light, clear broth. Served with sesame dipping sauce and an array of fresh vegetables.

Name: Donkey Burger
Description: A unique Beijing street food featuring slow-braised donkey meat sandwiched in a crispy flatbread. The meat is tender, flavorful, and surprisingly lean.

Name: Beijing Yogurt
Description: A traditional Beijing-style fermented milk drink sold in ceramic jars. Creamy, slightly tart, and refreshing — the perfect way to finish a Beijing meal.

Name: Dongpo Braised Pork
Description: A famous Hangzhou dish of pork belly slowly braised until caramelized and deeply flavorful. Melt-in-your-mouth tender with a glossy, reddish-brown color.

Name: West Lake Fish in Vinegar
Description: A delicate Hangzhou dish of freshwater fish poached and served with a sweet and tangy vinegar sauce. Light, refreshing, and perfectly balanced.

Name: Longjing Shrimp
Description: Fresh river shrimp stir-fried with premium Longjing (Dragon Well) tea leaves. The tea imparts a subtle, fragrant aroma that perfectly complements the sweet shrimp.

Name: Beggar's Chicken
Description: A whole chicken stuffed with aromatics, wrapped in lotus leaves, and baked in a clay shell. When cracked open, it releases an intoxicating aroma and reveals incredibly tender meat.

Name: Hangzhou-Style Braised Bamboo Shoots
Description: Tender spring bamboo shoots braised in a light soy-based sauce. Simple yet elegant, showcasing the natural sweetness of fresh bamboo shoots.

Name: Sweet & Sour Pork
Description: Crispy battered pork tenderloin coated in a glossy sweet and sour sauce. Golden in color, with a perfect balance of tangy and sweet flavors.

Name: Braised Sea Cucumber with Scallion
Description: A luxurious Shandong banquet dish featuring sea cucumber braised until tender in a rich, savory sauce with fragrant scallions. Silky texture with deep umami flavor.

Name: Dezhou Braised Chicken
Description: A renowned Shandong dish of whole chicken slow-braised in a complex marinade of soy sauce, spices, and rock sugar. The meat falls off the bone effortlessly.

Name: Shandong-Style Dumplings
Description: Plump, hand-pleated dumplings filled with juicy pork and fresh Chinese cabbage. Pan-fried to a golden crisp on the bottom, steamed to perfection on top.

Name: Scallion Pancake
Description: Crispy, flaky flatbread layered with fragrant scallions and sesame oil. Pan-fried until golden brown, each bite is crispy on the outside and tender within.

Name: Lion's Head Meatballs
Description: Oversized, tender pork meatballs braised with leafy greens in a rich, savory broth. The name symbolizes reunion and happiness, and the dish delivers deep, satisfying flavor.

Name: Yangzhou Fried Rice
Description: The gold standard of Chinese fried rice. Each grain is separate and perfectly coated, tossed with shrimp, char siu, egg, and peas for a colorful and satisfying dish.

Name: Squirrel-Shaped Mandarin Fish
Description: A stunning presentation dish where a whole fish is cross-cut, battered, deep-fried, and drizzled with sweet and sour sauce. The crispy fish pieces fan out like a squirrel's tail.

Name: Crystal Pork Trotter
Description: Pork trotter slowly braised until the skin becomes translucent and gelatinous. Served cold in slices, the texture is firm yet tender with a clean, savory taste.

Name: Wensi Tofu
Description: A masterpiece of knife skills: soft tofu is cut into thousands of hair-thin strands and floated in a delicate, clear broth. A testament to the refinement of Huaiyang cuisine.

Name: Soup Dumplings
Description: Shanghai's iconic specialty. Each dumpling has a paper-thin wrapper generously filled with seasoned pork and a burst of rich, savory broth. A gentle bite releases the soup.

Name: Shanghai-Style Braised Pork Belly
Description: Pork belly cubes braised in a dark, glossy sauce of soy sauce, rock sugar, and Shaoxing wine. The fat is rendered to a silky, melt-in-your-mouth texture.

Name: Pan-Fried Pork Buns
Description: Shengjian mantou: fluffy buns filled with seasoned pork, pan-fried until the bottom is golden and crispy. A sprinkle of sesame seeds and scallions completes the addictive snack.

Name: Scallion Oil Noodles
Description: A deceptively simple yet deeply satisfying Shanghai classic: chewy noodles tossed in aromatic scallion-infused oil and soy sauce. Minimalist perfection in a bowl.

Name: Shanghai Smoked Fish
Description: Fish fillets deep-fried and then marinated in a sweet-savory soy-based sauce. Served cold as an appetizer, the flavor is rich, sweet, and deeply satisfying.

Name: Di San Xian
Description: A beloved Dongbei home-style dish. Eggplant, potato, and green pepper stir-fried together in a savory sauce. The three vegetables complement each other perfectly.

Name: Guo Bao Rou
Description: Pork loin slices coated in potato starch, double-fried to a golden crisp, and coated in a tangy, sweet-and-sour sauce. Crispy outside, tender inside.

Name: Dongbei Pork and Pickled Cabbage Stew
Description: A hearty winter stew of tender pork belly, blood sausage, and tangy pickled Napa cabbage simmered together. Rich, warming, and deeply comforting.

Name: Dumplings with Sour Cabbage
Description: Hand-pleated boiled dumplings filled with tangy pickled Napa cabbage and seasoned pork. A Dongbei New Year essential that bursts with flavor in every bite.

Name: Dongbei Cold Noodles
Description: Chewy cold noodles served in a tangy, icy broth with sliced cucumber, pickled radish, and a hard-boiled egg. The perfect refreshing meal for a hot summer day.

Name: Steamed Fish Head with Diced Chilies
Description: A dramatic Hunan dish: a whole fish head steamed under a mountain of bright red diced chilies and fermented black beans. The meat is tender, sweet, and intensely spicy.

Name: Chairman Mao's Red-Braised Pork
Description: Pork belly cubes braised in a dark caramelized sauce with star anise and chili. A dish so beloved by Chairman Mao that it became synonymous with Hunan cuisine.

Name: Stir-Fried Smoked Beef with Chilies
Description: Thinly sliced smoked beef stir-fried with an abundance of fresh green and red chilies. Smoky, spicy, and incredibly aromatic — Hunan comfort food at its finest.

Name: Hunan-Style Spicy Frog
Description: Tender frog legs stir-fried with a generous amount of fresh chilies, garlic, and ginger. The meat is succulent and absorbs the fiery, aromatic sauce beautifully.

Name: Stinky Tofu
Description: A polarizing street food icon: fermented tofu deep-fried until golden and crispy, served with a spicy, garlicky sauce. Pungent aroma, addictive flavor, and crispy texture.

Name: Buddha Jumps Over the Wall
Description: A legendary Fujian dish of shark fin, abalone, sea cucumber, and other premium ingredients slow-simmered in a rich broth. The aroma is said to be so enticing it could tempt a vegetarian monk.

Name: Fujian Oyster Omelette
Description: Plump, fresh oysters folded into a savory egg batter with sweet potato starch, creating a crispy exterior and a gooey, flavorful interior. A beloved Fujian street food.

Name: Lychee Pork
Description: Pork pieces cut and fried to resemble lychee fruit, then coated in a vibrant red sweet and sour sauce. A beautiful presentation dish with a delightful balance of flavors.

Name: Fujian-Style Wonton Soup
Description: Delicate wontons with paper-thin skins filled with seasoned pork and shrimp, floating in a crystal-clear, umami-rich broth. Light, elegant, and deeply satisfying.

Name: Braised Eel with Angelica
Description: Tender eel braised in a rich, aromatic broth with Chinese angelica root and goji berries. A nourishing dish prized for its warming properties and delicate flavor.

Name: Xinjiang Lamb Skewers
Description: Chunky lamb cubes marinated with cumin, chili flakes, and salt, then grilled over charcoal until smoky and charred. The aroma alone is enough to make your mouth water.

Name: Xinjiang Big Plate Chicken
Description: A massive platter of braised chicken with potatoes, bell peppers, and hand-pulled belt noodles, all swimming in a rich, spicy sauce. A hearty feast meant for sharing.

Name: Xinjiang Hand-Pulled Rice
Description: Fragrant rice cooked with tender lamb, carrots, and raisins, infused with cumin and other Central Asian spices. Each grain is separate and bursting with flavor.

Name: Xinjiang Naan Bread
Description: Large, round flatbread baked in a traditional tandoor oven, topped with sesame seeds. Crispy on the outside, soft and chewy inside — the perfect companion to any Xinjiang meal.

Name: Yunnan Crossing-the-Bridge Noodles
Description: A legendary Yunnan dish: a bowl of scalding hot broth is served with an array of raw ingredients that you cook at the table. The story goes that a wife crossed a bridge daily to bring her husband this meal.

Name: Yunnan Steam Pot Chicken
Description: Chicken steamed in a special Yunnan clay pot with a central chimney. The steam condenses inside, creating an intensely flavorful and clear broth without adding any water.

Name: Yunnan Mushroom Hot Pot
Description: A celebration of Yunnan's famous wild mushrooms: matsutake, porcini, and more simmered in a light, aromatic broth. Earthy, fragrant, and utterly unique.

Name: Yunnan Rice Noodles with Minced Pork
Description: Chewy rice noodles topped with a savory minced pork sauce, pickled vegetables, and crushed peanuts. A quick, satisfying meal bursting with contrasting textures and flavors.

Name: Siu Mai
Description: Open-topped steamed dumplings filled with seasoned pork and shrimp, topped with a dot of crab roe. A dim sum classic with a satisfyingly firm yet juicy texture.

Name: BBQ Pork Buns
Description: Fluffy, white steamed buns filled with sweet and savory char siu (BBQ pork) in a glossy sauce. The contrast between the soft bun and the rich filling is pure comfort.

Name: Egg Tarts
Description: Buttery, flaky pastry shells filled with a silky, sweet egg custard and baked until golden. A beloved dim sum dessert with Portuguese-Macanese origins.

Name: Cheung Fun (Rice Noodle Rolls)
Description: Silky, slippery rice noodle sheets rolled around shrimp, beef, or char siu, drizzled with sweet soy sauce. A dim sum staple with a wonderfully smooth texture.

Name: Phoenix Claws (Chicken Feet)
Description: Chicken feet deep-fried, then braised in a savory black bean sauce until the skin is fall-apart tender. A dim sum delicacy prized for its rich, gelatinous texture.

Name: Lanzhou Hand-Pulled Noodles
Description: One of China's top ten noodle dishes. Hand-pulled noodles are springy and smooth, served in a crystal-clear, flavorful beef broth with thin slices of radish and fresh cilantro.

Name: Biangbiang Noodles
Description: Extra-wide, hand-pulled belt noodles from Shaanxi, topped with a fiery mix of chili flakes, garlic, and sizzling hot oil. Named after the loud slapping sound made during preparation.

Name: Chongqing Noodle Soup
Description: A fiery bowl of thin wheat noodles swimming in a complex, oil-based broth with Sichuan peppercorns, chili oil, minced pork, and pickled vegetables. Addictively spicy.

Name: Dandan Noodles
Description: Thin noodles topped with a rich, spicy sesame-peanut sauce, minced pork, and preserved vegetables. Named after the bamboo pole (dan) that street vendors used to carry their wares.

Name: Beef Noodle Soup
Description: A Taiwanese classic: tender braised beef chunks in a rich, aromatic broth with chewy wheat noodles and pickled mustard greens. Deeply flavorful and soul-warming.

Name: Sichuan Spicy Hot Pot
Description: A bubbling cauldron of fiery chili oil and Sichuan peppercorns, ready for you to cook your own selection of meats, vegetables, and tofu. A communal dining experience that's both social and delicious.

Name: Chongqing Spicy Hot Pot
Description: An intensely spicy and numbing hot pot from Chongqing, where the broth is so thick with chilies and peppercorns that it's almost black. A true test of spice tolerance.

Name: Coconut Chicken Hot Pot
Description: A lighter hot pot style from Hainan: fresh coconut water serves as the broth, with tender chicken pieces and an array of fresh seafood and vegetables. Sweet, clean, and nourishing.

Name: Mushroom Hot Pot
Description: A vegetarian-friendly hot pot with a rich, earthy mushroom broth as the base. Served with an assortment of fresh mushrooms, leafy greens, tofu, and root vegetables.

Name: Buddha's Delight
Description: A traditional Buddhist vegetarian dish combining an array of vegetables, tofu, mushrooms, and vermicelli in a light, savory sauce. Colorful, nutritious, and deeply satisfying.

Name: Stir-Fried Seasonal Greens with Garlic
Description: Fresh seasonal leafy greens (such as morning glory, bok choy, or Chinese broccoli) wok-fried with aromatic garlic. A simple, healthy side dish that lets the vegetables shine.

Name: Mapo Tofu (Vegetarian)
Description: A vegetarian version of the Sichuan classic, using mushrooms in place of minced meat. Silky tofu cubes simmered in a spicy, aromatic sauce that's just as satisfying as the original.

Name: Vegetable Spring Rolls
Description: Crispy, golden spring rolls filled with shredded cabbage, carrots, mushrooms, and glass noodles. Served with sweet chili dipping sauce for a perfect crunchy appetizer.

Name: Mango Pudding
Description: Silky, creamy mango pudding made with fresh mango puree and coconut milk. Topped with a drizzle of evaporated milk and fresh mango cubes for a refreshing tropical dessert.

Name: Red Bean Paste Pancake
Description: Crispy, golden pan-fried pancakes filled with sweet, smooth red bean paste. The contrast between the crunchy exterior and the warm, sweet filling is pure comfort.

Name: Osmanthus Jelly Cake
Description: A delicate, translucent jelly cake infused with the floral fragrance of osmanthus flowers and a hint of goji berry. Light, refreshing, and as beautiful as it is delicious.

Name: Sesame Balls
Description: Chewy glutinous rice balls coated in sesame seeds and deep-fried until golden. The crispy exterior gives way to a soft, slightly sweet interior filled with lotus paste or red bean.

Name: Jasmine Green Tea
Description: Premium green tea leaves scented with fresh jasmine blossoms, producing a delicately fragrant, smooth brew. The perfect palate cleanser between courses.

Name: Plum Juice
Description: A traditional Chinese beverage made from smoked plums, hawthorn, and rock sugar. Sweet, tangy, and slightly smoky — the perfect refreshing drink on a hot day.

Name: Bubble Milk Tea
Description: The globally beloved Taiwanese drink: creamy milk tea with chewy tapioca pearls. Rich, sweet, and satisfyingly fun to drink with an extra-wide straw.

Name: Herbal Tea
Description: A traditional cooling herbal tea blend of chrysanthemum, honeysuckle, and other Chinese herbs. Slightly bitter, slightly sweet, and believed to reduce internal heat.
